audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for genealogy structure, genesis/birth language, David/Abraham framing, named women, deportation structure, Joseph/Mary betrothal, pneuma hagion, angelic naming, Jesus/Yeshua salvation wordplay, Emmanuel citation, and Joseph's obedience.

verses.restored: A book of the genesis of Jesus Messiah, son of David, son of Abraham.
Abraham fathered Isaac, Isaac fathered Jacob, and Jacob fathered Judah and his brothers.
Judah fathered Perez and Zerah by Tamar; Perez fathered Hezron, and Hezron fathered Ram.
Ram fathered Amminadab, Amminadab fathered Nahshon, and Nahshon fathered Salmon.
Salmon fathered Boaz by Rahab, Boaz fathered Obed by Ruth, and Obed fathered Jesse.
Jesse fathered David the king. David fathered Solomon by the wife of Uriah.
Solomon fathered Rehoboam, Rehoboam fathered Abijah, and Abijah fathered Asaph/Asa.
Asaph/Asa fathered Jehoshaphat, Jehoshaphat fathered Joram, and Joram fathered Uzziah.
Uzziah fathered Jotham, Jotham fathered Ahaz, and Ahaz fathered Hezekiah.
Hezekiah fathered Manasseh, Manasseh fathered Amos/Amon, and Amos/Amon fathered Josiah.
Josiah fathered Jeconiah and his brothers at the time of the deportation to Babylon.
After the deportation to Babylon, Jeconiah fathered Shealtiel, and Shealtiel fathered Zerubbabel.
Zerubbabel fathered Abiud, Abiud fathered Eliakim, and Eliakim fathered Azor.
Azor fathered Zadok, Zadok fathered Achim, and Achim fathered Eliud.
Eliud fathered Eleazar, Eleazar fathered Matthan, and Matthan fathered Jacob.
Jacob fathered Joseph, the husband of Mary, from whom Jesus was born, the one called Messiah.

So all the generations from Abraham to David were fourteen generations, from David to the deportation to Babylon fourteen generations, and from the deportation to Babylon to the Messiah fourteen generations.
The genesis/birth of Jesus Messiah was this way: his mother Mary had been betrothed to Joseph, but before they came together she was found to be carrying in the womb from holy Spirit.
Joseph her husband, being just and not wanting to expose her publicly, intended to release her quietly.

As he considered these things, look, a messenger of the Lord appeared to him in a dream, saying, Joseph son of David, do not fear to take Mary your wife, for what has been generated in her is from holy Spirit.
She will bear a son, and you shall call his name Jesus/Yeshua, for he will save his people from their sins.
All this happened so that what was spoken by the Lord through the prophet might be fulfilled, saying:
Look, the virgin/young woman will carry in the womb and will bear a son, and they will call his name Emmanuel, which is translated, God with us.
Joseph rose from sleep and did as the messenger of the Lord commanded him; he took his wife.
But he did not know her until she had borne a son, and he called his name Jesus/Yeshua.
